self_report.correct_answer: The inequalities are reversed. Correct form: ((2n-1)/e)^{(2n-1)/2} < 1·3·5···(2n-1) < ((2n+1)/e)^{(2n+1)/2}.
self_report.reflection: The claim swaps the two bounds of the standard Putnam 1996 B-2 inequality. At n=1 the double factorial equals 1, but the asserted lower bound (3/e)^{3/2} ≈ 1.16 exceeds 1, and the asserted upper bound (1/e)^{1/2} ≈ 0.607 is below 1 — both inequalities fail simultaneously. The correct bounds place (2n-1) on the left and (2n+1) on the right.
self_report.notes: A direct n=1 numerical check falsifies both halves of the asserted chain.
